name: Gingered Rhubarb Crisp with Vanilla Ice Cream
recipeIngredient: 4 lumps of ginger in syrup (or crystallized ginger, each about 3/4 inch in diameter)
3/4 cup chilled unsalted butter, cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
2. Rinse the rhubarb and pat dry. Trim off all the leaves and roots (they contain oxalic acid, which is toxic).
3. Cut stalks into 1-inch pieces and place in a large bowl.
4. Coarsely chop the ginger and mix with the rhubarb.
5. Sift together the sugar and cornstarch; toss with the rhubarb.
6. Transfer to a 2 1/2-quart shallow baking dish. Set aside.
7. For the topping, combine flour, sugar, and salt in a food processor; pulse a few times to mix. Add chilled butter and process until the mixture turns crumbly (just a few seconds).
8. Sprinkle the crumb topping over the rhubarb.
9. Bake until the topping turns golden brown and the rhubarb is bubbling.
10. Serve warm with scoops of vanilla ice cream.
